includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Contamination Removal Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive database of contamination removal parameters, safety data, and laser cleaning specifications

contaminant.name: Environmental Dust Layer
contaminant.description: Environmental dust, it comprises fine particulates from air. This contamination forms by settling on surfaces after atmospheric exposure, and thus creates universal thin layers on all materials. Distinct from thicker residues by its light adhesion and variable buildup, it clings evenly yet reaccumulates quickly, thus poses removal challenges through mechanical means, so laser cleaning ablates selectively and restores cleanliness effectively.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Multiple toxic compounds detected: Carbon Monoxide, Carbon Dioxide, Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons (PAHs) - requires enhanced protection
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: N95 or P100 respirator for particulate control, standard ventilation. WARNING: Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons (PAHs), Formaldehyde - known carcinogen(s), minimize exposure

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (12 ACH) for particulate control
